seoTitle: Starpower News | Product Releases, Company Updates | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Latest news and updates from Starpower Semiconductor including new product releases, technology announcements, and company developments.

news.title: Starpower Expands SiC Module Portfolio with New 1200V Series
news.summary: Starpower announces the expansion of its SiC module portfolio with new 1200V devices targeting EV charging and renewable energy applications.
news.content:
Starpower Semiconductor has announced the expansion of its silicon carbide (SiC) module portfolio with the introduction of a new series of 1200V devices. These new SiC modules feature improved efficiency and higher switching frequencies, making them ideal for electric vehicle charging infrastructure, solar inverters, and high-frequency power supplies.

The new series includes modules rated at 1200V with current ratings from 50A to 200A, featuring RDS(on) as low as 6mΩ. These devices are manufactured using Starpower's advanced SiC MOSFET technology, which provides superior performance compared to traditional silicon IGBT modules.

Key features of the new series include:
- Ultra-low RDS(on) for reduced conduction losses
- Fast switching capability up to 100kHz
- High-temperature operation up to 175°C junction temperature
- Integrated NTC temperature sensors
- Industry-standard EasyPACK and 62mm packages

The new devices are available for sampling now, with production quantities available in Q2 2026. Starpower provides comprehensive technical support including reference designs, application notes, and thermal simulation models.
news.author: Starpower Semiconductor

news.title: Starpower IGBT Modules Surpass 1 Million Units in EV Applications
news.summary: Starpower celebrates milestone of 1 million IGBT modules deployed in electric vehicle powertrains and auxiliary systems.
news.content:
Starpower Semiconductor announced today that its IGBT modules have surpassed 1 million units deployed in electric vehicle applications. This milestone demonstrates the reliability and performance of Starpower modules in the demanding automotive environment.

The 1 million units include modules used in:
- EV traction inverters (main drive motors)
- Onboard chargers and DC-DC converters
- HVAC compressor drives
- Power steering and brake systems
- Battery management systems

Starpower's automotive-qualified modules undergo extensive testing including AEC-Q101 qualification, temperature cycling (-40°C to +150°C), and mechanical shock testing. The modules are used by leading Chinese EV manufacturers and have accumulated billions of kilometers in real-world driving conditions.

"This milestone reflects the trust that EV manufacturers have placed in our products," said the VP of Sales at Starpower. "We continue to invest in automotive-qualified products to support the rapid growth of the EV market."

Starpower is expanding its automotive product line with new SiC modules for next-generation 800V EV platforms, expected to launch in Q3 2026.
news.author: Starpower Semiconductor

news.title: Starpower Modules Enable 99% Efficiency in Next-Gen Solar Inverters
news.summary: New solar inverter designs using Starpower SiC modules achieve record-breaking 99% CEC efficiency.
news.content:
Starpower Semiconductor announced that its SiC power modules have enabled a new record in solar inverter efficiency. A leading solar inverter manufacturer has achieved 99% California Energy Commission (CEC) weighted efficiency using Starpower's GDS100M12B1 SiC modules in a 150kW three-phase string inverter design.

This achievement represents a significant improvement over typical silicon IGBT-based designs which achieve 98-98.5% efficiency. The higher efficiency translates to:
- 0.5-1% more energy harvest over the system lifetime
- Reduced cooling requirements
- Smaller and lighter inverter designs
- Lower levelized cost of energy (LCOE)

The design uses Starpower's 1200V SiC MOSFET modules in a T-type three-level topology switching at 32kHz. The high switching frequency enabled by SiC technology allows for smaller magnetics and filters, reducing overall system cost despite higher module cost.

"Starpower's SiC modules provided the performance and reliability we needed to achieve this efficiency milestone," said the CTO of the solar inverter company. "The technical support from Starpower's team was instrumental in optimizing the design."

Starpower continues to work with leading solar inverter manufacturers to push the boundaries of power conversion efficiency.
news.author: BeiLuo Electronics

news.title: Starpower Opens New Advanced Packaging Facility
news.summary: Starpower inaugurates new state-of-the-art power module packaging facility to meet growing demand.
news.content:
Starpower Semiconductor has officially opened its new advanced packaging facility in Jiaxing, China. The new facility represents a $200 million investment and increases Starpower's power module production capacity by 50%.

The facility features:
- Class 1000 cleanroom for die attach and wire bonding
- Automated production lines with robotic handling
- Advanced DBC (Direct Bonded Copper) substrate manufacturing
- Comprehensive reliability testing laboratory
- Environmental chambers for automotive qualification testing

"This new facility positions us to meet the rapidly growing demand for power modules in EVs, renewable energy, and industrial applications," said Starpower's CEO at the opening ceremony. "The advanced automation ensures consistent quality while scaling production."

The facility has obtained IATF 16949 automotive quality certification and ISO 14001 environmental certification. It will focus on producing high-power IGBT modules, SiC modules, and custom power modules for automotive and industrial customers.

The new facility is expected to create 500 new jobs and will be fully operational by Q2 2026.
news.author: Starpower Semiconductor

news.title: Starpower Achieves ASPICE Level 2 Certification for Automotive Development
news.summary: Starpower receives ASPICE Level 2 certification, demonstrating mature automotive software and hardware development processes.
news.content:
Starpower Semiconductor is pleased to announce that it has achieved ASPICE (Automotive SPICE) Level 2 certification for its automotive product development processes. This certification recognizes Starpower's commitment to quality in automotive electronics development.

ASPICE is the international standard for assessing development processes in the automotive industry. Level 2 certification indicates that Starpower has established and consistently follows managed processes for:
- System and software requirements analysis
- Architecture and design
- Implementation and integration
- Verification and validation
- Configuration management and quality assurance

This certification, combined with Starpower's existing IATF 16949 quality management certification and AEC-Q101 product qualifications, positions Starpower as a fully qualified supplier for automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers.

"ASPICE Level 2 certification is a significant milestone that demonstrates our capability to develop automotive products according to industry best practices," said Starpower's Quality Director. "It enables us to participate in more automotive programs and expand our presence in the EV market."

Starpower is targeting ASPICE Level 3 certification by 2027 as it continues to enhance its development processes.
news.author: Starpower Semiconductor
